<<POS:05672232>> ase(e.longName)),e.description='an external toxin pump'

original: Bright Water’s external toxin pump hasn’t yet been given a name, and you can see why. The bulbous central cylinder hangs from a harness of industrial straps, meant to be looped around the wearer’s torso. Two support tubes, glowing neon green, stretch out to a pair of metallic silver cuffs. The inner edge, where they would press against skin, is lined with a strangely undulating white leather or hyper-dense fabric, you’re not sure which.\n\nThe attached development notes indicate that the system can painlessly extract toxins from the wearer’s blood without the need for needles or an intravenous connector. A later note indicates a desire to reduce the efficacy of the system, so as not to negatively impact sales of Bright Water’s pharmaceutical products.
context: <<POS:05672267>> external toxin pump",e.tooltip='Bright Water’s external toxin pump hasn’t yet been given a n'

original: Feruze wings down from a perch above as you approach the taxi, hefting a dufflebag up over her shoulder. “So? You ready to get this party started?” You nod, checking your own [pc.weapon][pc.cl mercenary |. Feruze grins toothily. |, though hopefully you won’t have cause to use it. ] “Alright, finally!” the big shark cheers, waving down one of the passing hover-cabs.[flags.FERUZE_CAME_INSIDE |“We’re gonna shake that little [rival.mf |bastard |bitch ] down for our kids, right?” You nod. “Absolutely.” |“Fuck, can’t believe that little shit’s making us go through all this effort. My rep’s gonna take a beating if I lose a Steele.” [flags.RIVAL_DANCE |“Yeah, I’m worried too...” |You grunt noncommittally. ] ] A taxi finally descends down to your level, side hatch popping open before it’s even stopped moving. A pair of clearly-intoxicated, giggling zaika stagger out flashing you chrome-framed grins and winking cybernetic eyes. Feruze all but shoves them out of the way and piles into the back seat. Guess that leaves you for the front. A much less augmented zaika is sitting in the driver’s seat, and when you hand her the credential chip, she whistles. “Whew, fancy pants on this offworlder! You sure you wanna go down there, though? Last I heard there’s a pretty big riot fixing to break out in the Farrow.” “A riot?” “Mmhm! Well, m’wife insists it’s a protest for the sake of the planet’s future, but the [targetFactionName] radio ain’t mincing words. Announcer says there’s a whole mess of anti-capital rebels trying to tear down the very fabric of our society for their misguided ideals. How awful.” You glance back at Feruze. “Mind turning that radio on while we fly down?” “Sure thing, spacer!” the cabby says, lifting off and fiddling with her console. You’re assaulted by a crackling of static, and then a sultry-smooth voice comes over the speakers. “-are continuing to block traffic into and around [targetFactionName]’s most important and profitable facilities in the Farrow Industrial Zone, preventing hundreds of workers from benefiting from their high-paying and benefit-laden jobs within. Our illustrious leader has already promised a swift end to these obstructions at any cost, but the class traitors have made it known that they will not be moved until their craven demands are met. We contacted the facility overseer for comment:” Another voice comes over the wire, rough as a gravel tumbler, though still doubtless a zaika. “A work week of less than sixty hours is frankly absurd and threatens the entire productivity cycle all of us break our backs to maintain. These selfish bitches can’t see the bigger picture — we’re playing catch up with the whole galaxy down here now. There’s no time for slacking off or crying about the cost to the environment. The sunward side burns away all the refuse from this facility anyway.” “And there you have it, darlings,” the announcers says. “Traitorous workers — and I use the term loosely — are threatening not only [targetFactionName] employee wages, but the standing of our entire world in the wider galaxy. Let’s all hope the security services will teach these thugs a lesson they’ll feel from their tips to their tails.” “Yeah, get ‘em!” the cabby cheers, punching the air. “Fuckin’ up my whole route, those assholes. You’re the first people wanting to go down there in days!” In her taxi, maybe. You glance out the window, watching the descent from the urban streets to the smoke-blanketed industrial zone below the Gyre. It’s just below, though, but also further afield than you’ve been before: your adventures so far have kept to the narrow band in the middle of the world-belting city, cozy in the perpetual twilight between the sun-scorched and frozen sides of the world. The taxi takes you sunward, far enough that you can see a golden haze glaring down even through the industrial smog. Even inside the sealed can of the transport, you can feel the heat starting to chew through the air con. Your driver fiddles with a dial, cranking it up, but it can’t keep pace with the sun’s fury as you near the hot side of the planet.

<> addButton(e++,"Next",(function(){clearAll(),feruze.showAll(),author("Savin"),output(textify(Tx||(Tx=(0,Bt.Z)([" “Hope you two brought some sunscreen. Especially you in back! You’re some kinda aquatic alien, right?” Feruze makes an uncomfortable noise, pulling a phial of someth...

original: “Hope you two brought some sunscreen. Especially you in back! You’re some kinda aquatic alien, right?” Feruze makes an uncomfortable noise, pulling a phial of something clear and greasy out of her duffle and starting to smear it over her scales. “Yeah don’t spend too much time outdoors down here,” your driver continues, “you’ll get suuuuper cancer. But I’m sure a [targetFactionName]-licensed doctor can fix you right up, oh yeah. But better to be safe than sorry, y’know, especially if you’re not used to the Dhaal sun. Not many people native to the planet are either!” She laughs, pulling you around the corner of a skyscraper and nosediving towards the ground. Once you’re angled towards the street, you can immediately see the so-called riot sprawling out on the streets: they weren’t kidding about this crowd of zaika blocking traffic! There’s got to be hundreds, maybe thousands, packed into the streets and marching on what you can only imagine is your target: a sprawling industrial park surrounded by tall concrete walls and belching black pillars of smoke into the air. You see all manner of signs poking out of the crowd, demanding better pay, better hours, better healthcare, better environmental controls... and they’re being met with a bristling line of security cyborgs and automated gun turrets protecting the sealed front gates. Your driver has to land quite a ways back, where the crowd’s thinned down enough that she isn’t crushing thirty people trying to land — though she seems to take some glee in making a bunch of protestors go running to get out of the way. Feruze shoves a credstick into the cabby’s hand and bails out, stretching her wings. The driver grins ear to ear, tucking the chit down the front of her shirt. “Thanks, big fish! Don’t dawdle out there in the sun, okay?” You give her a thumbs up and hop out, following Feruze into the crowd. A few zaika, all dressed in dirtied neon-lined leather and work jumpers, scowl at you — but they scatter when Feruze gets her hackles up, bearing her fangs. “Alright,” you say, pulling the big woman back. “Let’s get to work.”

original: [flags.FARROW_TALK_GUARDS |You step up to the guards again, keeping your face stony as several auto-cannons turn their attention to you. The officer you spoke to watches you approach with one hand on her cigar, and the other on the butt of a big-bore pistol slung on her hip. “What is it, starborn? Headquarters is breathing down my neck — I shouldn’t even be talking to you with these greasers ready to ram the gates.” |You step out of the crowd, approaching the line of armed guards. Several cyber-aug’d zaika snap their rifles up to their shoulders, covering you and Feruze in laser dots. Your companion bares her fangs, mace-tail thrashing as if to challenge a whole gun line of guards and turrets. You put a hand on her[pc.heightRange 84 | shoulder | hip ], holding her back. One of the guards steps forward, wearing a red beret and a uniform with different stripes on its shoulders, raises a hand up to the others. “What do you want, offworlders? Can’t you see there’s a riot going on?” “We noticed,” you tell her. “I’m guessing the gate’s not opening until they break up, right?” The soldier makes another motion to her guards, and the lasers deactivate. You take that as permission to step closer, so you aren’t shouting to be heard over the crowd. While you approach, the zaika lights up a fresh cigar, taking a deep drag off it and watching you with wary, apprising synthetic eyes. “Listen, star-born. I don’t know why you’re here, but this [targetFactionName]-brand industrial zone is locked down until this crowd disperses. I’ve got Peers trapped inside. I’ve got the overseer breathing down my neck telling me to resolve this. I’ve got rioting dissidents that want to tear my girls limb from limb. But between you and me, I’d still rather not turn a couple hundred greasers into Gyre cheese. Get my meaning?” Feruze snorts. “You want us to solve your problems for you, tit-bitch?” “Tit-bitch?” “Ugh. [pc.name] we don’t have time for this!” ]

original: “We can disperse this crowd for you,” you suggest. Not like you’re getting inside if the status quo endures. Place is locked down tighter than a princess’s chastity belt. Feruze crosses her arms. “We can?” Something approaching a smile crosses the commander’s features. “I don’t care how you do it, just get rid of these whiny tailbrains. I’m sure the overseer has some hazard pay for freelancers she’ll be happy to dispense if you do.” You nod, turning towards the rioters and resting a hand on your [pc.weapon]. The rioters near the front of the herd gasp, trying to move back and make space, but there’s a solid wall of zaika bodies behind them. The zaika at the head of the crowd, a woman with a cheap-looking cyber arm and a bullhorn, furrows her brow over a pair of mirrorshades. “See how even the aliens are zaibatsu lickboots!” she shouts, amplified painfully by the megaphone. “The very heavens are as corruptible as the overseer and her minions! They’re just another cancer on this planet!” A furious howl of anguish and hatred radiates through the assembled masses. You see crowbars and chains begin appearing in zaika hands, gripped in calloused, scarred hands. Feruze sighs and activates the macehead on her tail, dropping into a fighting stance. “Let’s make this quick.” You intend to.

original: [flags.FARROW_TALK_CROWD |“You again?” the zaika with the bullhorn scowls. “What corruption have you come to spread now, alien?” |You gesture for Feruze to hang back as you head towards the mob. There’s a solid wall of zaika bodies pressed together in the street, shouting and chanting at the security forces facing them. The rioters’ hands are all either hoisting slogan-laden signs demanding better pay and safer working conditions, or thrust skyward in fists that shake to the tempo of their sing-song demands. Still, you’ve been around the block enough to pick out the glint of metal tucked under capes and jackets. So far none of them have drawn weapons, though, and so you approach with an even expression and your own hand away from your [pc.weapon]. While it’s hard to determine at a glance if there’s any meaningful organization to the riot, there’s certainly one zaika that stands out from the crowd: a lean, malnourished-looking woman with a cheap-looking cybernetic arm attached at the bicep. She’s leading the chants, shouting “What do we want? Living wage! When do we want it? Last shift!” You step up when she stops for breath, waving a hand to get her attention. The woman scowls and lowers her horn, fixing you with mirror-shaded eyes. “An offworlder, here in the Farrow? Haven’t you and your alien zaibatsu done enough harm on Dhaal already?” she grows suspiciously. “You’re all in bed with the machine!” ]

original: “So aside from the obvious, what’s got you all worked up?” you ask, surveying the massive crowd. “There’s hundreds of zaika down here, maybe thousands, all shoulder to shoulder for... what?” “For what? For freedom!” she shoots back. “For the freedom to live. Each of us has lived our lives under the boot of the zaibatsu, working obscene hours without reprieve and earning barely enough to get by. And now — now these tailsuckers are docking our collective pay to put in safety measures that they took away last year to cut costs. Look at what they’re doing to us!” The woman clenches a metal fist, hard enough to make the metal creak. Several other zaika repeat the gesture, showing off chrome arms, legs, tails, and more. “Zaika lives are just as replaceable as the gears in the machines!” the leader growls. “Maybe more, since the overseer doesn’t have to put up a down payment for workers.” “So what happened to you, exactly?” The leader bristles, rubbing her thumb over her scarred bicep. “I’m a lathemeister, fourth grade. I made the best glass in forty sectors, everything from hovercar windshields to test tubes. When you aliens showed up, though, the overseer increased my quota by fifty percent and cratered my off time to nothing — I had to suffer pay docks just to sleep. What do you think’s going to happen when you run a factory like that, huh? Your workers start falling asleep at the helm, no matter how many stims they buy from the company store. I fainted at the controls cuz I hadn’t slept in seventy hours, and then next thing I know I’m screaming hoarse cuz my arm’s in the lathe. And you know what [targetFactionName]’s response was, offworlder? The fucker fined me for damaging their equipment! My mother had to sell herself to the Utopian brothel on Khalesh street just to afford this shitty rust of mine!” [pc.intelligenceRange 75 |Even for a mega-corp that’s beyond the pale. Something tells you this woman’s blowing things way out of proportion... |God damn. That’s brutal even for a mega-corp. ] Several other zaika pat their comrade’s shoulder, grumbling about how awful [targetFactionName] is and how they’ve suffered, too. Just keeping an ear out tells you several of these ladies have had similar workplace accidents, or are afraid they’ll soon face something even worse as safety standards inside decline.

original: “What’s it going to take to get you all to go home?” you ask. No harm, right — even if you can’t exactly meet their demands yourself. Several of the zaika on the front lines scowl; some behind them shout anti-alien obscenity. The leader, though, raises her robotic fist to silence them. “You can see our demands on our signs, offworlder. Unless you mean to buy up all of [targetFactionName], then you aren’t responsible for our woes. Not directly, at least, though the overseer and her mistress have certainly gotten cozy with you aliens.” “Surely there’s something I can do,” you say. “Come on. Those guards have hair triggers as it is. You can’t stay here much longer.” Though the woman you’re talking to stays steely-calm, you catch several of the other zaika glancing worriedly at the guards and their turrets. “Unless you can put a wedge into the zaibatsu machine and make the overseer see just how broken this system is — no. If you want us gone, alien, then help us destroy the tools of oppression! If the overseer won’t meet our demands, then we’ll show her the real power of united workers!” “You... want me to help you fight the guards?” She eyes you cautiously. “I say you have a better chance than we do, if it comes to that. You have weapons, shields; we have crowbars and clothes that are barely more than tatters. And even if we could, I don’t want to hurt the troops: they’re so programmed by their overseer that they don’t know how to breathe if somebody doesn’t give them an order to. They’re victims too.” Yeah, there’s no way these guys would win in a fair fight. Not with that row of gun turrets up on the walls auto-targeting everybody. Feruze nudges you with her tail. “Come on, let’s crack these corpo heads already and get this over with.” [pc.cl mercenary |Cracking skulls is great, but a frontal assault against a fortified position like this is just asking for trouble. You need to shut down those turrets at least. |Even with all your kit, though, trying to fight your way through all those turrets seems ill-advised... ]

context: <> addButton(e++,"Search Area",(function(){clearAll(),feruze.showAll(),author("Savin"),output(textify(Cx||(Cx=(0,Bt.Z)([" You glance over at Feruze. “Let’s see if we can find a way to tackle the security system.” “From out here? They’d have to be a whole new kind of stupid if ...

original: You glance over at Feruze. “Let’s see if we can find a way to tackle the security system.” “From out here? They’d have to be a whole new kind of stupid if there is.” [pc.isBimbo |“Naaaah, it’ll work itself out.” |You nod. “They were expecting poor undereducated zaika, not well-equipped aliens, when they built this place.” ] Feruze shrugs and steps off towards some of the nearby buildings outside the compound; you take the other side of the street, glancing between the towering bases of the skyscrapers surrounding the complex. You doubt there’s anything of note in the buildings — those could be owned and accessed by anybody — but the mega-city of Dhaal is so old and dense that the infrastructure, at least, probably hasn’t been updated much if at all over the years. And your contact said this facility itself was pretty damn old. A bit of searching, and you find just what you’re looking for: a manhole. Womanhole, considering the native species? Either way, there’s a service tunnel in a nearby alley, and the lock’s easy enough to dispatch with a blow from your [pc.meleeWeapon].[var1 | There’s no limits to the uses for a trusty rock! ] The smell down there’s atrocious, but you slip down anyway, using the light on your Codex to illuminate the tunnel running parallel to the street above. Sure enough, there’s a host of cables running along the ceiling and walls, safely restrained from contact with the flowing water beneath. You follow the cabling for a short while, tracing fingers on worn-down old plastic coverings, until you find just what you’d been hoping for: a junction box. [var0 |No way a [silly |spoopy |secret ] corporate blacksite doesn’t have its own internal power supply, but a surge might be enough to fry some of their systems. From the high peaks of the Meridian to the most tenebrous corners of Dhaal, change comes at the end of a gun. Territory is seized, hands are greased, and galactic megacorporations cut their deals and shore up the winning side in clandestine meetings. Enriched beyond their wildest dreams, [favoredFactionName] begins a campaign of subjugation, meticulously eradicating their rivals at all levels of society. All-out war rages under the eternal night sky, in the chessboard slums of the Gyre, every living soul playing out the end of one world and the building of a new one. On every mission you are joined by one partner and no other: a golden-skinned beauty with mechanized wings and jet-packs for flight, the first of a long line of [silly |next-generation special forces |new operator models ]. She takes to the skies with an irrepressible mania too violent to be simply conditioned away. XO-FZR, however, is pliant to the smooth, quiet voice in her ears, same as you. She lives for the thrill of combat and the fun that always comes after. You are an inseparable duo wreaking havoc across the Gyre and a few times in the Meridian. [favoredFactionName], over the course of long, difficult months in which the status quo is sent hurtling into chaos, gradually seizes power and crushes the opposition. VIP removal is your specialty. Demolition is your partner’s. Together, you are unstoppable, and with hundreds of sorties to your names, you now stand at either side of [favoredFactionLeader] in a grand audience hall.
